text: Data validation is the process of determining whether a dataset is suitable for use. By formalizing the constraints that determine valid data we ensure that we communicate clearly and unambiguously, automate tedious workflows and finally that we gain insight into the structure of the validation process itself. In this blog post we give a formal representation of data validation and investigate its properties in depth. We give a formal definition of the validation process along with a mathematical representation of the properties it fulfills. Based on this representation we identify three data quality constraints and show how they can be used to classify data quality constraints in an increasing order of structural complexity. We investigate the logical subtleties behind combining validation rules into a validation framework. Including data validation redundancies and inconsistencies.
explanation: The text is coherent and technical, but relies on very generic, formulaic phrasing and contains a stray sentence fragment, patterns that are common in AI-generated text.
